parsed_review.summary: CP00-299 is a descriptor-only RP09 (CRM And Business Development) bootstrap pack adding packages/crm, contracts/crm-core-contract.json, and the rp09:crm-core:validate gate; 150 units, risk class C, range RP09.P00.M00.S01-RP09.P01.M08.S05. Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the production-readiness posture is supported and not overclaimed: the manifest and command-evidence both report production_ready=false / implemented_pending_review, and the Hermes production_ready_candidate=true is correctly derived from coverage.valid && foundation.valid. Coverage is internally consistent and self-enforcing — phase counts (P00=52, P01=98=150), twenty descriptor sections, deliverable distribution (impl 104/contract 3/security_audit 6/ui 19/fixture 3/test 9/hermes 3/claude 3=150), and per-section row titles all match the plan pack and the SCOPE*/MODEL* arrays, enforced by validateCrmCoreCp299Coverage and validateCrmCoreCp299ScopeContractFoundationDescriptor. The contract JSON mirrors the generated descriptor (validator asserts deepEqual on descriptor, pack binding, requirements, and no-write attestation; historical_pack_bindings ends at CP00-299). Tests are wired into npm test (package.json:10) and the validator into rp09:crm-core:validate (package.json:62); command-evidence shows every gate at exit 0. No-write/no-real-data/no-runtime attestations are present and enforced; authority boundaries hold (Claude is read-only and non-final-approver; no enterprise trust claimed from local validation); Hermes is used only as a deterministic descriptor (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. Review-hardening readiness shows a passed auth preflight, prompt within size limit (2488/25600 bytes), no full diff/source in prompt, and read-only tools only. One P3 cosmetic finding: a Claude-review question string says "twenty-one" sections where the pack defines twenty (no logic impact, not embedded in contract). No P0/P1/P2 issues; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Claude review packet question text says 'twenty-one' descriptor sections but the pack defines twenty
findings.details: In createCrmCoreCp299ClaudeReviewPacket the second review question begins 'Do the twenty-one descriptor sections (P00 M00-M10 scope foundation cycle and P01 M00-M08 model foundation cycle ...)'. The pack actually defines twenty sections: P00 M00-M10 = 11 micro-phases and P01 M00-M08 = 9 micro-phases = 20. This is confirmed by CRM_CORE_CP299_REQUIREMENTS.required_section_rows (20 keys), the case set section_count=20, the assertions in packages/crm/test/model.test.js (caseSet.section_count===20 and required_section_rows length===20), and packages/crm/README.md which correctly states 'twenty descriptor-only sections'. The wording lives only in a human-readable question string and is not embedded in contracts/crm-core-contract.json, so no validator, gate, or coverage computation depends on it. Cosmetic descriptor-text inconsistency only.
findings.recommendation: Change 'twenty-one descriptor sections' to 'twenty descriptor sections' in packages/crm/src/validators.js:210 to match the actual section count and the README wording. No code or contract changes are required.
